title: Network egress allowlist for agent Bash to prevent data exfiltration
source: OWASP LLM Top 10 (2025) — LLM06 Excessive Agency & LLM02 Sensitive Information Disclosure; codebase gap: no egress controls, most roles have Bash
description: Agents with Bash access (backend/frontend/fullstack/devops/tester Morty's) can make arbitrary outbound requests (curl, wget, git remotes, package installs from any index). With no egress allowlist, a prompt-injected agent can exfiltrate repo contents or secrets to an attacker host. Add a domain allowlist enforced in the guardrail hook, defaulting to the known-good set (github.com, pypi.org, npmjs.com, the project's own APIs).
proposed_changes: Add scan_command_egress(command) to security_utils.py that extracts URLs/hosts from Bash commands (curl, wget, nc, git remote, pip/npm --index-url) and checks them against a CTO_EGRESS_ALLOWLIST env-configurable set (sane defaults: github.com, pypi.org, files.pythonhosted.org, registry.npmjs.org, anthropic.com). Wire it into the hooks.py guardrail: unknown host in 'block' mode → exit 2; in 'warn' mode → audit_log_security_event 'egress_denied' + roro event. Document the env var in the security section of the README/config.
